text.title: rag Failure Modes
text.summary: Four observable antipatterns when building production RAG.

antipattern.description: Random or score-ascending context ordering buries top-relevance chunks in the middle.
antipattern.reason: LLM attention is biased to beginning and end. Detector: faithfulness drops as top_K grows beyond 5. Fix: enforce r4-context-order — highest score first.

antipattern.description: Model emits plausible-sounding citations that do not match any retrieved chunk.
antipattern.reason: Without post-generation citation validation, hallucinations look authoritative. Detector: citation.validated=false rate > 0. Fix: parse citations, intersect with retrieved chunk metadata; flag answer if any unvalidated.

antipattern.description: Shipping a RAG pipeline without an MRR / faithfulness measurement.
antipattern.reason: Quality regressions go undetected until user complaints surface them. Detector: launch checklist missing eval block. Fix: enforce r6-launch-gate — block deploy without MRR>0.7 and faithfulness>0.9.

antipattern.description: Trying to attach filename / page / section metadata after the index is already built.
antipattern.reason: Requires full re-embed → expensive + downtime. Detector: metadata fields blank in stored docs. Fix: enforce r1-chunk-and-metadata at ingestion; rebuild from source if missing.
